title: Acianthera platystachys
taxonomy: Acianthera platystachys is a species of orchid native to Brazil. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the Orchidaceae family, Acianthera platystachys is classified as a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Epidendreae. The genus Acianthera is a relatively small group of orchids, with Acianthera platystachys being one of the many species within it. Acianthera platystachys is often classified as an epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants for support.

intro: Acianthera platystachys, also known as the 'Long-stemmed Acianthera', is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and combat infections.
